jokes.text: It's not coffee, it's hot Pepsi.
jokes.setup: Lisa tattles that Bart is drinking coffee
jokes.punchline: Bart's defense reveals something arguably worse
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Bart's twisted logic - technically not lying while making things worse. Classic Simpsons absurdism.

jokes.text: You're the worst husband in the world! / Yeah, well, I wish I'd married Patty and Selma.
jokes.setup: Marge calls Homer the worst husband
jokes.punchline: Homer escalates by wishing he'd married her sisters (who hate him)
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Homer's spite makes him say something nonsensical since Patty and Selma despise him.

jokes.text: I wish I'd married Lenny and his friend.
jokes.setup: Homer wishes he'd married her sisters
jokes.punchline: Marge escalates to wishing she'd married two men, one unnamed
jokes.explanation: Marge matching Homer's absurdity is unexpected, plus the casual 'and his friend' is perfectly dismissive.

jokes.text: Recording that fake fight was a great idea. Now the kids won't bother us during our morning snuggle.
jokes.setup: We hear parents fighting badly
jokes.punchline: Reveal it's fake to get privacy for intimacy
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that recontextualizes everything we just heard. Smart structural comedy.

jokes.text: Dear Lord, thank you for the physical intimacy we are about to enjoy. And as always, have fun watching.
jokes.setup: Homer praying before intimacy
jokes.punchline: Implies God voyeuristically watches them
jokes.explanation: Peak Homer - earnest faith combined with completely inappropriate theology. Dark but character-perfect.

jokes.text: Trust me, Bart. It's better to walk in on both your parents than on just one of 'em.
jokes.setup: Kids about to walk in on parents
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's dark wisdom about parental situations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy that implies Milhouse has walked in on single-parent situations. Adult humor delivered by a kid.

jokes.text: But what will I eat at midday?
jokes.setup: Nelson demands lunch money
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's formal, literal interpretation of 'lunch'
jokes.explanation: Classic Milhouse - overly formal and literal in casual situations. Mild but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: I can't solve all your problems. Just hand over the money.
jokes.setup: Milhouse worries about eating
jokes.punchline: Nelson's matter-of-fact dismissal while robbing him
jokes.explanation: Nelson's practical approach to bullying - acknowledging he can't help while continuing to harm.

jokes.text: Thanks, wads, and I hope to see you both Saturday. / Come to my party or die.
jokes.setup: Nelson politely thanks them and mentions Saturday
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a death threat disguised as party invitation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - mixing politeness with threats. The tonal whiplash from cordial to menacing works well.

jokes.text: He makes you look his baseball cards and tell him they're good. They're not good.
jokes.setup: Bart explains why he hates Nelson's parties
jokes.punchline: Mundane awkwardness of forced praise for bad cards
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about awkward social obligations. The flat delivery sells it.

jokes.text: Individually, we are weak like a single twig. But as a bundle, we form a mighty faggot.
jokes.setup: Martin gives inspirational speech about unity
jokes.punchline: Uses archaic meaning of 'faggot' (bundle of sticks) in modern context
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels - Martin's vocabulary creates unintended double meaning.

jokes.text: Hell, no, I won't mow! / Willy, you're fired with complete loss of pension.
jokes.setup: Kids chant 'Hell no, we won't show'
jokes.punchline: Willie mishears and chants about mowing, gets fired instantly
jokes.explanation: Great throwaway gag. Willie's mishearing leads to immediate consequences, perfectly timed.

jokes.text: Well, if no one else jumped off the Empire State Building, would you not jump?
jokes.setup: Standard parental logic about peer pressure
jokes.punchline: Marge accidentally reverses the usual saying
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal of familiar saying that completely changes the meaning. Classic Simpsons wordplay.

jokes.text: Kind of...
jokes.setup: Marge's backwards logic about jumping off building
jokes.punchline: Bart considers it might actually make sense
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to previous joke. Bart's contemplative response extends the comedy.

jokes.text: Lisa, you're never gonna get a husband by being sarcastic. / All right, no husband. / You're getting a husband!
jokes.setup: Homer warns Lisa about sarcasm affecting marriage prospects
jokes.punchline: Lisa accepts no husband, Homer immediately reverses position
jokes.explanation: Homer's parental panic when his threat backfires. Good character moment showing his contradictions.

jokes.text: You think I wouldn't rather be living nude in the forest like some ancient pagan, just dancing around playing the pan flute?!
jokes.setup: Homer explains everyone has to do things they don't want
jokes.punchline: His alternative fantasy is oddly specific pagan lifestyle
jokes.explanation: Completely unexpected and specific fantasy that reveals Homer's hidden desires. Perfect absurdist escalation.

jokes.text: Uh-oh, do I hear a dial tone? 'Cause this party is off the hook!
jokes.setup: Bart sees Nelson's sad empty party
jokes.punchline: Forced enthusiasm with phone pun that doesn't work
jokes.explanation: Bart's awkward attempt to be positive. The bad pun is intentionally cringey, showing his discomfort.

jokes.text: Is Highway 88 backed up? / Oh, yeah, it's a parking lot out there. / Of course, that explains everything. Stupid Highway 88!
jokes.setup: Nelson needs excuse for why no one came
jokes.punchline: Both cling to obviously fake traffic excuse
jokes.explanation: Mutual delusion as both characters desperately maintain the fiction. Nelson's anger at imaginary highway is good.

jokes.text: Maybe I should come back a little later. / Don't go. My party peeps are en route, I swear!
jokes.setup: Performer wants to leave empty party
jokes.punchline: Nelson's desperate slang ('party peeps') while begging
jokes.explanation: Nelson trying to use hip slang while desperately pleading. Character vulnerability showing through tough exterior.

jokes.text: I ordered them to.
jokes.setup: Nelson wonders why nobody came to his party
jokes.punchline: His genuine confusion about why threats didn't work as invitations
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson line showing his complete misunderstanding of social interaction. Threats as invitations.

jokes.text: How'd you afford this kickass party? / Carnival ride hit me on the head.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about party funding
jokes.punchline: Nelson's casual mention of head injury as income source
jokes.explanation: Perfectly casual delivery of absurd explanation. The matter-of-fact tone sells the ridiculous premise.

jokes.text: Hey! It's my birthday bud!
jokes.setup: Day after party, Nelson sees Bart at school
jokes.punchline: Nelson assumes one good interaction equals permanent friendship
jokes.explanation: Nelson's desperation for friendship leading to instant attachment. Character-driven humor about loneliness.

jokes.text: An entire case of pool cue chalk. We can make the tip of anything blue.
jokes.setup: Nelson gives Bart a present
jokes.punchline: Completely useless gift with overly enthusiastic explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfectly random gift that shows Nelson has no idea what friends want. The specific enthusiasm is funny.

jokes.text: I got a lot on my plate. I've got a big report due on the Nile River - topic sentence, bibliography, page numbers - it's nuts.
jokes.setup: Bart tries to avoid friendship with Nelson
jokes.punchline: Lists basic homework requirements as if they're overwhelming
jokes.explanation: Bart treating minimal homework as impossible workload. Good character moment showing his academic priorities.

jokes.text: You made me get water on my cheek.
jokes.setup: Nelson is clearly crying from rejection
jokes.punchline: His denial that tears are just 'water on cheek'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson - can't admit to crying so describes it clinically. Tough guy maintaining facade while vulnerable.

jokes.text: Let's go over to the Sunshine pre-school and wail on toddlers. / Yeah, we'll jump 'em while they're napping.
jokes.setup: Bullies need new targets after Bart is protected
jokes.punchline: Escalate to attacking defenseless sleeping children
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation showing how cowardly the bullies really are. The napping detail makes it more absurd than disturbing.

jokes.text: But your mother smells like coconut.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks for bedtime story
jokes.punchline: Homer's distracted by Marge's scent
jokes.explanation: Homer's priorities showing - distracted by wife during parenting duty. Character-appropriate but mild.

jokes.text: Oh, yeah, this is that fantasy book that even grown-ups like. Broken, lonely grown-ups.
jokes.setup: Homer recognizes the fantasy book series
jokes.punchline: Judges adult fans as psychologically damaged
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about fantasy genre demographics. Homer's judgmental tone while he's about to get obsessed himself.

jokes.text: I like to come up here and make fun of the sunset. Hey, gas ball, you suck!
jokes.setup: Nelson explains his roof activity
jokes.punchline: Bullies even the sun with scientifically accurate insult
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson moment - his need to bully extends to celestial objects. 'Gas ball' shows he knows science but still insults it.

jokes.text: I mean, a shark can't stop swimming or it'll blow up.
jokes.setup: Nelson explains he can't stop being a bully
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong shark biology delivered with confidence
jokes.explanation: Nelson's confident delivery of completely wrong information. Shows his tendency to mix up facts while being philosophical.

jokes.text: The sleeves were torn off by wild dogs.
jokes.setup: Nelson gives Bart his signature vest
jokes.punchline: Casual explanation of dramatic origin story
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery of absurd backstory. The casual tone makes the wild dogs more funny than frightening.

jokes.text: So, Scotty, how do you like school? / Closed!
jokes.setup: Marge tries to make conversation with tough kid
jokes.punchline: One-word dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Economy of language - complete shutdown of conversation in one word. Shows tough kid attitude efficiently.

jokes.text: You must read ahead. Fiction is your passion! / Lady, you're nice, but I'm married.
jokes.setup: Fantasy characters urge Homer to read
jokes.punchline: Homer interprets literary seduction as romantic advance
jokes.explanation: Homer's literal interpretation of metaphorical seduction. Shows his inability to understand literary concepts.

jokes.text: Thanks for the spoiler there, big mouth.
jokes.setup: Homer reads death of character aloud
jokes.punchline: Book character complains about having their death spoiled
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about spoilers. Character awareness of their fictional status creates humor.

jokes.text: Because my chest is cold and my arms aren't.
jokes.setup: Milhouse questions why Bart wears the Nelson vest
jokes.punchline: Bart's completely practical explanation for fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Bart's matter-of-fact response avoids admitting to friendship. Good logical reasoning for illogical garment.

jokes.text: I was waiting to smash this frog with you, and it peed in my hand.
jokes.setup: Nelson waited for Bart to do something together
jokes.punchline: The 'something' is animal cruelty, and it backfired
jokes.explanation: Nelson's idea of friendship activity is disturbing but gets comeuppance. Dark humor with justice.

jokes.text: Look what you made me do!
jokes.setup: Nelson gets upset at perceived betrayal
jokes.punchline: Blames Bart for his own self-destructive reaction
jokes.explanation: Classic bully psychology - refusing responsibility for own actions. Character-appropriate but not surprising.

jokes.text: I just hit a major marshmallow vein. I think it goes all the way to the bottom.
jokes.setup: Offering ice cream to guests
jokes.punchline: Describes ice cream container like a mining operation
jokes.explanation: Creative metaphor comparing ice cream discovery to mining. The enthusiasm for marshmallows is endearing.

jokes.text: It's not true-- they're brave, 'cause they're strong.
jokes.setup: Homer mentions that 'all bullies are cowards'
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts the conventional wisdom with flawed logic
jokes.explanation: Homer's backwards logic that completely misses the point of the saying. Unhelpful parenting through faulty reasoning.

jokes.text: You're a nut bar, a whack job! A kooka-dooka!
jokes.setup: Bart rejects Nelson's friendship
jokes.punchline: Escalating series of silly insults ending with made-up word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with the invented 'kooka-dooka' as the climax. Shows Bart running out of real insults.

jokes.text: I've set up a trust. It bypasses the inheritance tax. Only till 2008. Look into it!
jokes.setup: Nelson threatens to kill everyone
jokes.punchline: Martin offers practical financial advice about death
jokes.explanation: Perfect Martin - even facing death threats, he's thinking about tax optimization. The specific date reference adds authenticity.

jokes.text: Time to do what I do best. Lie to a child!
jokes.setup: Homer realizes the book will make Lisa sad
jokes.punchline: Proudly announces his parenting strategy
jokes.explanation: Homer's proud declaration of bad parenting. The enthusiasm for deception is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Mustache power: activate!
jokes.setup: Homer improvises book ending
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous superhero-style power activation
jokes.explanation: Homer's childish approach to fantasy adventure. The specific 'mustache power' is appropriately silly.

jokes.text: They went to Star Wars Land and fought star wars.
jokes.setup: Lisa asks what happened to other characters
jokes.punchline: Homer's lazy, nonsensical explanation
jokes.explanation: Homer's complete creative bankruptcy. 'Fought star wars' shows his inability to even explain his own story.

jokes.text: I hate milk! It comes from cow wangs!
jokes.setup: Nelson mentions stealing milk for Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's disgusted misunderstanding of cow anatomy
jokes.explanation: Bart's childish misconception about dairy production. The word 'wangs' makes it funnier and more kid-appropriate.

jokes.text: Man lips! Skinner loves Bart! Skinner loves Bart!
jokes.setup: Principal Skinner gives Bart CPR
jokes.punchline: Kids misinterpret life-saving as romantic
jokes.explanation: Standard schoolyard misunderstanding of adult emergency response. Predictable but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: You mean...? / Exactly. You're barf to me now.
jokes.setup: Bart thinks Nelson saved him out of friendship
jokes.punchline: Nelson clarifies Bart means nothing to him now
jokes.explanation: Nelson's emotional whiplash - from life-saving heroism to casual dismissal. 'You're barf to me' is perfectly harsh.

jokes.text: Don't say his name. In your mouth, it sounds like dirt!
jokes.setup: Marge mentions Greystash casually
jokes.punchline: Homer's over-the-top grief reaction to character death
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic overreaction to fictional death. The 'sounds like dirt' metaphor is appropriately theatrical.

jokes.text: No man should outlive his fictional wizard. No man!
jokes.setup: Homer continues grieving the book character
jokes.punchline: Treats fictional death as violation of natural order
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist escalation. The tragic tone applied to ridiculous situation is perfectly Homer. Very quotable.
